scope.diff_summary: Converted the first Workbench large-file governance candidates into open known-risk work packages, documented grouping and daily dry-run rules, made known-risk dry-run selection dependency-first, and added tests for open large-file risk package integrity and bounded dry-run selection.
gates_reviewed: Four large-file risks are concrete, open, and include scope, owned files, acceptance gates, and evidence.
Server route extraction depends on server test sharding, and projection domain extraction depends on projection test sharding.
Daily dry-run selects one bounded risk and keeps preflight_only true and closeout_completed false.
Required closeout mode is expected to fail while the new large-file risks remain open.
Headless orchestrator work is explicitly deferred to LFG-P5 rather than mixed into Workbench P3/P4 scope.
Write mode remains rejected before ledger or lock mutation.

notes: DeepSeek sharded review returned PASS with no blocking findings. Non-blocking feedback was addressed by documenting the headless-scope rationale, making dry-run selection dependency-first, and removing live-ledger coupling from runner tests. Final delta review returned PASS with no blocking or non-blocking findings.
